title: Lorian Nod

{{Quote|I've seen your heart, and I know how empty it is.|Lorian Nod talking to Dooku|<ref name="The Shadow Syndicate">{{SWGTCG|set=The Shadow Syndicate|cardname=Dark Temptation}}</ref>}}
'''Lorian Nod''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] serving the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] as a member of the [[Agricultural Corps/Legends|Agricultural Corps]] following his expulsion from the [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple's]] [[Jedi academy|academy]] in the decades preceding the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. Nod eventually turned away from the Order to become a [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] before achieving elected office on the planet [[Junction V]].

==Biography==
===Early life===
A [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] in [[102 BBY/Legends|102 BBY]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Junction V]] in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]], Lorian Nod was [[Jedi training/Legends|trained]] in the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] at the [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]]. As an [[Jedi Initiate/Legends|Initiate]] in the [[Jedi academy|Temple's academy]], Nod was sorted into a [[Clan (Jedi)/Legends|clan]] of his peers where he became quick friends with fellow student, [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]]. As a student, Nod possessed no personal [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], and had plans on becoming a [[Jedi Consular]] upon achieving knighthood. Training for over a decade in each other's company, Nod was jealous of Dooku when he was selected in [[89 BBY]] by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Thame Cerulian]] to continue his training as a [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]]. In the days following Dooku's apprenticeship, the pair investigated Cerulian's [[Jedi Temple dormitory|private chambers]] while the [[Corellian Jedi]] was away on a mission.<ref name="LoT" />
[[File:Noddingoff.jpg|thumb|left|Nod and Dooku are instructed by Yoda]]
Eager to learn about the Jedi Master, the pair discovered several documents and journals, including one which contained information drawn from a [[Sith holocron/Legends|Sith holocron]] that was stored in the [[Holocron Vault/Legends|Holocron Vaults]] of the [[Jedi Archives/Legends|Temple Archives]]. Fascinated by the device, Nod attempted to convince Dooku to help him gain access to the device. Citing the regulations of the Archives and the Vaults, Dooku refused his friend and chastised him for broaching the subject. Undeterred, Nod slipped into the Vaults and stole the [[Dark Holocron]] in secret, accessing its hidden knowledge within his own chambers. Sickened by the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]] which was imbued in the holocron, Nod came to Dooku for help. As Dooku scolded his friend for stealing the ancient artifact, Jedi Councilor [[Oppo Rancisis/Legends|Oppo Rancisis]] was drawn to the darkness emanating from the room and confiscated the holocron. Nod accused Dooku of stealing the holocron and Dooku, shocked by the accusation, was unable to say anything.<ref name="LoT" />

In the following days, the Council placed the two former friends on opposing teams during a group exercise through Coruscant. The teams, tasked with obtaining a [[Muja fruit/Legends|muja fruit]] from the [[Old Galactic Market]] in the [[Senate District/Legends|Senate District]], eventually crossed paths and Dooku and Nod engaged in a fight. Upon returning to the Temple, the Council called the pair to a meeting, during which Dooku, who was infuriated with his friend's betrayal, explained that Nod was the only one to seek out the holocron and he alone bore the guilt. With this knowledge, the High Council deemed Nod unfit to continue along the Jedi Path at the Temple not for stealing the holocron, but for lying and implicating a friend. Dooku would maintain his apprenticeship with a mild warning to watch his pride.<ref name="LoT" />

Following the High Council's decision, the [[Council of Reassignment/Legends|Council of Reassignment]] placed Nod in the [[Agricultural Corps/Legends|Agricultural Corps]], a division of the [[Jedi Service Corps/Legends|Order's Service Corps]], where the failed Jedi would stay for the next thirteen years. Eventually, Nod abandoned his post with the Service Corps and became the leader of a band of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] [[Pirate/Legends|raiders]] along the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]].<ref name="LoT" />

In [[76 BBY/Legends|76 BBY]], Nod was secretly hired by the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth Tenebrous/Legends|Darth Tenebrous]] to kidnap [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Blix Annon]].<ref name="Darth Plagueis">[[Darth Plagueis (novel)|''Darth Plagueis'']]</ref> During the kidnapping, he encountered an older Dooku, who by then had obtained the rank of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] and taken on the training of sixteen-year-old [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]]. Nod managed to capture the two Jedi on [[Von-Alai]] but was unable to keep them; their escape nearly resulted in Nod's death after a brief encounter with an enraged Dooku. Before Dooku could kill him, Padawan Jinn calmed his master and Nod was arrested, tried, and sent to [[Prison/Legends|prison]].<ref name="LoT" />

Released around [[52 BBY/Legends|52 BBY]] after many years imprisoned, Nod returned to his homeworld where he rose to become leader of a group called the [[Junction 5 Guardians|Guardians]], a group meant to protect the world from the rogue government of [[Delaluna]], Junction V's [[Moon/Legends|moon]]. With the people in fear of a destructive weapon called the [[Annihilator (superweapon)|Annihilator]], which Nod propagated as being in the possession of the Delalunan government, Nod assumed control of the planet's government for eight years. It was not until Qui-Gon Jinn, now a Master, and his own apprentice [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] were passing through Junction V on a stopover during a mission and learned about the supposed threat of the Annihilator that Nod's power was questioned. The two Jedi soon discovered that Nod's rule was built on lies and propaganda and with the help of a woman named [[Cilia Dil]], the Jedi managed to overthrow the leader and once more send him to prison.<ref name="LoT" />

===Separatist crisis===
Following a prison sentence of nearly two decades, Nod reconsidered his life and changed his outlook on things. Once released, he returned to Junction V to truly help out its citizens rather than do things for his own gain. Elected through a proper election, Junction V, along with Delaluna and the [[Bezim system/Legends|Bezim]] and [[Vicondor system/Legends|Vicondor systems]], created the [[Station 88 Spaceport]] under Nod's governance. As the lives of his constituents began to prosper, the galaxy broke out in all out war, as the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] began the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. Several weeks<ref name="The Essential Reader's Companion">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref> after the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|start of the war]], the Confederacy sought to claim the Station 88 spaceport for themselves, given its strategic [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] location. Dooku, now the Sith Lord Darth Tyranus, contacted Nod, telling him that with his support, he could convince the leaders of the other worlds controlling the spaceport to join the Confederacy of Independent Systems. However, Nod sought to do the exact opposite, and contacted [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] of the Jedi Order, hoping to redeem himself for all of the terrible crimes he had committed. Nod informed the Order that he would be attending a meeting with the other rulers and Dooku on the Separatist-held [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Null/Legends|Null]] in which he would formally decline the Confederacy's offer.<ref name="LoT" />

The High Council sent Obi-Wan Kenobi, now a Knight, and his apprentice, [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] to Null to meet with Nod in secret as a way to [[Spy/Legends|spy]] on Dooku during the summit. Shortly after they arrived, the leader of Delaluna, [[Samish Kash]], was [[Assassin/Legends|assassinated]] by the Confederacy as a means to draw out the Jedi which Dooku suspected were in attendance. Kenobi, uneasy with the forced alliance with Nod because of their past encounter suspected that Nod was indeed a turncoat and a Separatist sympathizer. After accusing Nod, Kenobi learned that the assassination attempt had been botched and that Kash was indeed alive. At the conference, the rulers of Junction V and its neighbors announced that they would not join the Separatist cause, causing Dooku to order their deaths. As the [[Droid/Legends|droids]] prepared to assassinate the leaders, the Jedi arrived to cut down the Separatist soldiers and save the leaders. Noticing Dooku flee during the battle, Nod followed his former friend into a secret [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] where he attempted to stop the Separatist leader from escaping. Sensing Nod's approach, Dooku stood in wait and [[Shiak|impaled]] his former colleague through the back with his [[Dooku's lightsaber/Legends|crimson lightsaber]], killing Nod.<ref name="LoT" />

===Remembered by Dooku===
Dooku would never forget Nod's betrayal from when he was a young Padawan. During the [[Battle of Coruscant/Legends|Battle of Coruscant]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], he briefly recalls Nod's betrayal.<ref name="LoT" />
